title: geitonoplesium cymosum subsp. asperum
intro: Geitonoplesium cymosum subsp. asperum, also known as the Australian arum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Colchicaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits such as reducing pain, fighting infections, and improving wound healing. Herbal preparations of Geitonoplesium cymosum subsp. asperum include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being. While more research is needed to fully understand its effects, the Australian arum has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
